name: Dominic Rapini
subject: A New Years Resolution for Connecticut Elections
body:
Let's resolve to bring trust to our elections

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/672a0e723680/when-we-vote-we-win-14761561?e=8763c56857)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| (**ACTION** : Forward this email to your local lawmakers)
---
|
---
| | | | | “Voting is the most precious right of every citizen, and we have a moral obligation to ensure the integrity of our voting process.” — Hillary Clinton
_**“Sorry, I couldn’t resist. Now we know why Hillary Clinton**_ _**won the Presidential Medal of Freedom.”**_
---
|
---
| **Securing the Vote:**
**A New Year’s Resolution for Trustworthy Connecticut Elections**
---
| | | | | | | | | |
---
| _By Dominic Rapini, American Businessman and Political Commentator_
As a proud, principled Republican and former candidate for Connecticut Secretary of State, my New Year’s resolution is clear: it’s time to restore trust in our electoral process through common-sense measures. Elections should reflect the people's will, not become a tug-of-war over loopholes and gray areas. Connecticut deserves better, and here’s how we can achieve that.
**IDs: The Key to Trust**
Let’s start with the basics. In a country where you need an ID to rent a car, buy a six-pack, or even enter some office buildings, why on earth don’t we require one to vote? Requiring government-issued identification to vote isn’t controversial—it’s common sense. 87% of Americans support voter ID laws.
But wait, there’s more! Starting May 9, 2025, the federal Real ID—that fancy one with the gold star—becomes the standard for accessing government buildings, courthouses, air travel, and even crossing borders. It’s like the new golden ticket, except instead of getting into Willy Wonka’s chocolate factory, it gets you through TSA. This isn’t just about elections; it’s about being a functioning citizen in the United States.
Connecticut should get ahead by helping citizens secure their Real IDs. Let’s make this a seamless part of the voting process. Picture this: a voter walks into town hall, flashes their Real ID (or gathers the necessary documents to get one), and walks out ready to vote and navigate the bureaucratic maze of modern life. It’s like killing two birds with one gold star.
Whether you’re voting in person or requesting an absentee ballot, a simple ID check can go a long way toward rebuilding confidence in the system. Shouldn’t ballot casting carry the same standard if IDs are required for everyday tasks? After all, no one wants their elections to be less secure than their Netflix account.
**Drop Boxes: Safe, Secure, and Sensible**
Ballot drop boxes were a pragmatic solution during the height of the pandemic, but their security remains a weak link in the chain. Leaving them unattended on sidewalks or street corners is an open invitation for misuse or tampering.
The solution is straightforward: move drop boxes indoors to government buildings, where they can be monitored under video surveillance. Access should be limited to business hours, just as you’d expect when paying taxes or renewing your driver’s license. This isn’t about making voting harder; it’s about making it more secure.
**Fixing the Frenzy of Same-Day Registration**
I’ve seen firsthand how chaotic same-day registration can be. On Election Day, registrars are expected to verify newly registered voters while simultaneously running a high-stakes election. It’s like drinking from a fire hose—and the result is often rushed, error-prone vetting that undermines the integrity of the process.
Here’s a compromise: Any same-day or Election Day registrations that cannot be fully vetted in time should be treated as provisional ballots. Once the voter's information is verified, these ballots can be counted in full. This ensures no legitimate voter is turned away but guarantees that shortcuts won’t compromise the system. We can’t afford to undermine confidence in our elections by rushing through this critical process.
Let’s be honest: voters have 364 days to register, so waiting until the last minute doesn’t look good. Encouraging procrastination only adds to the chaos, which the current system invites. Worse yet, we shouldn’t see busloads of college students arriving at polling stations at the eleventh hour, clutching boxes of pizza and an agenda they’re only halfway invested in. Voting should reflect a genuine connection to the local community—not just a rushed stop on the way back to campus.
We should also address situations where individuals who don’t meet Connecticut’s residency or citizenship requirements still want to vote. For example, a voter who just moved here and failed to cast an absentee ballot in their former hometown could be issued a federal ballot. This would allow them to vote for federal offices—like the president or congressional representatives—without participating in state or local elections where they have no meaningful connection. Local races should be decided by residents who understand and are invested in the community—not by “carpetbagger” voters passing through.
By treating unvetted registrations as provisional ballots, encouraging timely registration, and creating a solution for out-of-state voters, we can bring much-needed order and integrity to Connecticut’s same-day registration process. It’s not about making voting harder; it’s about making it fair, secure, and grounded in the community.
**Residency Requirements: The Georgia Gold Standard**
Georgia has emerged as a model for election integrity. It requires new residents to live in the state for at least 30ing. Connecticut should adopt a similar rule. New voters should be required to prove their residency with documentation such as days before vot a utility bill, lease agreement, or updated driver’s license.
You shouldn't be voting here if you’re not invested enough in Connecticut to establish residency. It’s as simple as that.
**One Voter, One Vote: Leave Ranked-Choice Voting Behind**
Ranked-choice voting (RCV) might sound trendy, but it’s a solution in search of a problem. This convoluted system confuses voters, dilutes results, and undermines confidence in elections. Worse, it can leave voters feeling like their ballots didn’t count.
Our democracy is built on a simple principle: one voter, one vote. Why complicate it? Let’s keep Connecticut’s elections straightforward and accessible.
**Hands Off Absentee Ballots: The Voter’s Responsibility**
Absentee voting is an essential option for many voters, and Connecticut has made it easy for everyone to access this convenience. Voters can request an absentee ballot online through the Secretary of State’s website or visit their local town clerk to make a request—hopefully while presenting a government-issued ID. These systems are designed to ensure accessibility, and that’s a good thing.
But accessibility comes with responsibility. While we want to make voting easy, we also need to emphasize the importance of accountability in the process. Voters must take ownership of their ballots, whether they’re voting in person or remotely.
Let’s face it: voting in person is always the safest way to ensure your vote is counted. In-person voting eliminates risks like insufficient signatures, ballots lost in the mail, or outright malfeasance. Even bad luck—such as your late ballot—can undermine your voice when you vote by mail. Once your ballot leaves your hands and enters the postal system, you lose control over its journey.
That’s why voters who can should prioritize casting their ballots in person. For those who must vote absentee, the process must remain secure and free from interference. Political operatives should never be allowed to touch an absentee ballot or application—it’s just too easy for trust to be broken when accountability isn’t clear.
Ultimately, whether voting in person or by mail, the goal is to ensure every legitimate vote is counted and every voter’s voice is heard.
**Voter Fraud: Zero Tolerance**
Voter fraud strikes at the heart of democracy, yet Connecticut’s response has been tepid at best. Every recent fraud case in our state has involved absentee ballots, which are typically mishandled by campaign operatives. This isn’t just illegal; it’s an affront to every honest voter.
To deter future fraud, we need stiff penalties: mandatory jail time for offenders and a lifetime ban on participating in campaigns or running for office. Let's make it clear that you don't get a second chance if you break the public's trust.
**A Trustworthy Future**
None of these proposals are radical; they’re rooted in common sense. By implementing these measures, Connecticut can create a system where voters—regardless of party affiliation—feel confident that their voices are heard and their votes are secure.
Let 2025 be the year we restore trust in our elections. It’s not just a resolution—it’s a responsibility.

Elections should reflect the people's will, not become a tug-of-war over loopholes and gray areas. Connecticut deserves better, and here’s how we can achieve that. Your opinion is vital:

[1) Do you SUPPORT Trump's Cabinet?](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zrlt55pk2cprn47fragktk2lr29307mrpq11d3l0_rp22sh2s8i6epj3c9hm9oj4cjh2bs)

subject: MAGA's dangerous deportation plan
body:
This is a threat to American values.
| | | | | | [](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2vzzew-rxruu2-kyd76rh9/)

# **MAGA's Dangerous Deportation Plan**
---
| | | | | [ ](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2vzzew-rxruu3-kyd76rh0/)
---
| | | | Hey friend, Donald Trump’s plan to deploy the U.S. military for mass deportations is not only reckless but a betrayal of the principles that define America. Even Republican Senator Rand Paul has denounced the idea, calling it a “huge mistake” and a blatant misuse of our military. Think about it: soldiers in uniform marching into cities, tasked with rounding up immigrants—including people who’ve built their lives here for decades, contributed to their communities, and raised families. It’s a dystopian image straight out of a nightmare, and one that has no place in a free and democratic society. For over 100 years, it’s been illegal to use the military in domestic law enforcement, and for good reason. America’s armed forces are meant to defend our country—not to police it. Deploying them in this way sets a chilling precedent and tarnishes their honor. It’s a gross overreach of executive power and a direct attack on the values of liberty and justice. Even Paul, a conservative and sometimes Trump ally, is warning against this disastrous plan. “I don’t see the military putting [a housekeeper] in handcuffs and marching her down the street to an encampment,” he said. And yet, that’s exactly the kind of scene Trump is pushing for. This is about more than just policy—it’s about who we are as a nation. Using the military to target undocumented immigrants is a dangerous step toward authoritarianism. It erodes trust, divides communities, and sets the stage for widespread fear and abuse. The fight to stop this starts now. It’s time to stand up for American values—compassion, fairness, and the belief that everyone deserves a shot at the American dream. Together, we must resist these draconian measures and ensure that no president uses our armed forces to carry out their personal vendettas. Let’s make our voices heard. Let’s fight for the America we believe in. [**_Our most powerful weapon right now is our microphone, so we need to use platforms around the country to hold MAGA accountable! Join us in our fight today._**](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2vzzew-rxruu4-kyd76rh1/)

name: History Facts
subject: A new ancient city was just discovered
body:
In the world of archaeology, advancements in technology have enabled discoveries that may never have happened otherwise, including that of an ancient Maya city. [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68cfmzx8zrtjre850av8vv2ivy6olotr9uunl61d9za0kshdfvluwzqo76ns09uhx7rdyxzx2h5bqbu9h9p5g49o1obxlpkfehdxezk9tubjwwnx83omzna5p6phdhx54j6418tu8y7gf3710&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [A new ancient city was recently discovered.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [Science & Industry](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| I n the world of archaeology, advancements in technology have enabled discoveries that may never have happened otherwise, including that of an ancient Maya city. Located under dense jungle canopy in the southeastern Mexican state of Campeche, the lost city was [rediscovered](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) in October 2024 using lidar, a laser radar technology.
---
Lidar works by sending out laser light pulses from an aircraft and measuring how long they take to bounce back — not unlike how some animals use echolocation. The information helps create detailed maps of the Earth's surface, showing things such as land shapes and structures. The site was discovered by researchers at Tulane University, but they didn’t deploy the radar themselves; they found a 2013 lidar survey of the area several pages deep into a Google search. The survey was initially done to measure carbon in Mexico's forests, and the results had never been studied by archaeologists before. Once the research team processed the data, it was clear to them what they were looking at.
The city, which the researchers named Valeriana after a nearby lagoon, had all the markings of [a capital city](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). It’s thought that upwards of 50,000 people may have called Valeriana home at its peak between 750 and 850 CE — a population second only to that of Calakmul, one of the largest ancient Maya sites. The researchers determined that Valeriana was once home to pyramids, ball fields, dense housing, causeways connecting distinct districts, and amphitheaters. The exact reason for its eventual abandonment isn’t clear, but archaeologists suggest that, not unlike other Maya cities, its dense population could not be sustained when faced with challenging conditions such as drought.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## The Maya ball game wasn’t just a sport — it was also political and spiritual.
The Maya are known for their ball game “pitz,” which was so central to their culture that some settlements, such as Chichén Itzá, had multiple courts dedicated to it. While the sport was indeed played for entertainment, it was also much more than that — it held a great deal of political and spiritual significance. Archaeologists [believe](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sppwbx&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) the players used the trunks of their bodies to propel a heavy rubber ball to the other side of the court. The game was often played to settle conflicts and territory disputes, and captives of war could be forced to play, with the leader of the losing team sometimes being sacrificed. Pitz was also thought to symbolize the eternal struggle between life and death, light and darkness, and even the movement of celestial bodies, with the court itself serving as a connection to the underworld.

clean_body: In the world of archaeology, advancements in technology have enabled discoveries that may never have happened otherwise, including that of an ancient Maya city. | I n the world of archaeology, advancements in technology have enabled discoveries that may never have happened otherwise, including that of an ancient Maya city. Located under dense jungle canopy in the southeastern Mexican state of Campeche, the lost city was in October 2024 using lidar, a laser radar technology. Lidar works by sending out laser light pulses from an aircraft and measuring how long they take to bounce back — not unlike how some animals use echolocation. The information helps create detailed maps of the Earth's surface, showing things such as land shapes and structures. The site was discovered by researchers at Tulane University, but they didn’t deploy the radar themselves; they found a 2013 lidar survey of the area several pages deep into a Google search. The survey was initially done to measure carbon in Mexico's forests, and the results had never been studied by archaeologists before. Once the research team processed the data, it was clear to them what they were looking at. The city, which the researchers named Valeriana after a nearby lagoon, had all the markings of . It’s thought that upwards of 50,000 people may have called Valeriana home at its peak between 750 and 850 CE — a population second only to that of Calakmul, one of the largest ancient Maya sites. The researchers determined that Valeriana was once home to pyramids, ball fields, dense housing, causeways connecting distinct districts, and amphitheaters. The exact reason for its eventual abandonment isn’t clear, but archaeologists suggest that, not unlike other Maya cities, its dense population could not be sustained when faced with challenging conditions such as drought. | Thank you for supporting our advertisers! | By the Numbers | | Year the first lidar prototype was built by Howard Hughes’ company 1961 | | Federal entities in Mexico (31 states and the capital city) 32 | Federal entities in Mexico (31 states and the capital city) 32 | | Years ago that Plato first mentioned the lost city of Atlantis 2400 | | Characters, both hieroglyphic and phonetic, in the Maya writing system ~800 | Characters, both hieroglyphic and phonetic, in the Maya writing system ~800 | | | Did you know? ---|---|--- The Maya ball game wasn’t just a sport — it was also political and spiritual. The Maya are known for their ball game “pitz,” which was so central to their culture that some settlements, such as Chichén Itzá, had multiple courts dedicated to it. While the sport was indeed played for entertainment, it was also much more than that — it held a great deal of political and spiritual significance. Archaeologists the players used the trunks of their bodies to propel a heavy rubber ball to the other side of the court. The game was often played to settle conflicts and territory disputes, and captives of war could be forced to play, with the leader of the losing team sometimes being sacrificed. Pitz was also thought to symbolize the eternal struggle between life and death, light and darkness, and even the movement of celestial bodies, with the court itself serving as a connection to the underworld. This isn’t just a turning point—it’s a victory waiting to be confirmed.
| | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JH0B1EGDR851H8C3NEYWF1G4_0)
---
| | | | Dear Friend, As President Trump nears the culmination of his remarkable political comeback, and as the US Senate prepares to confirm Trump’s cabinet picks, including Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s appointment to lead the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), we have an extraordinary opportunity to ensure a seismic shift in our public health agencies.
2025 is the beginning of a brighter future for the health of every single American.
Simple put, you and I will make the difference. Together, our national grassroots movement to Make America Health Again, and our commitment to transparency, science-based solutions, and prioritizing people over profits is exactly what America needs.
Restore Trust, Accountability, Well-Being
For decades, federal health agencies have operated with zero accountability, putting the health and security of families last while giving precedence to corporate interests.
In just days, those who have abused and misused their authority will be replaced.
WIth the massive mandate given to President Trump, you and I can lean into that and call for bold new reforms. MAHA Alliance is poised to tackle years of mismanagement head-on, bringing compassionate and courageous leadership that prioritizes every American family.
Your Role in this Moment
The momentum is undeniable, and the first step is doing everything we can to ensure Kennedy’s confirmation. That is the start of a movement to transform public health and it will require all of us.
Together, we can show the US Senate and the nation that we are bigger and more powerful than they or their friends in the media know—our fight is about giving Americans a healthcare system they can trust and rely upon.

Let’s stand together as we usher in a new era of leadership, transparency, and accountability in public health.

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 1/7/2025
body:
Congress certifies Trump’s 2024 election victory, clearing path to Oval Office
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hey everyone,
Here's a summary of today's newsletter:
🏔️**Here in Colorado...** Brita Horn, a former Routt County treasurer, a fire chief and grassroots and civic leader, has announced her candidacy for [chair of the Colorado Republican Party](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). Meanwhile, grassroots organizations like the [Smart Wolf Policy](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) group are rallying to challenge Colorado’s controversial wolf reintroduction law.
_*Not featured but worth reading:_ As the [2025 legislative session](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) approaches, Colorado is preparing for significant debates on issues ranging from health care reform to labor laws and environmental regulations. The upcoming session promises to impact nearly every sector of the state’s economy, including new challenges for businesses and efforts to reshape union policies.
🇺🇸**On the National Stage...** Vice President Kamala Harris [certified Donald Trump’s victory](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) in the 2024 presidential election during a joint session of Congress. Meanwhile, Canadian Prime Minister [Justin Trudeau announced his resignation](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) after a contentious tenure marred by economic struggles and disputes with President-elect Trump. This departure has left Canada’s political landscape in flux ahead of its next general election.
🗣️ **Commentary Spotlight...** A [letter from a rural Colorado farmer](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) reflects on the decline of the American family farm and the societal shifts that have contributed to economic and cultural changes in rural areas. Additionally, Cory Gaines encourages citizens to hold politicians accountable by [filing campaign finance complaints](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) when violations are suspected. Lastly, an extra read: Jon Caldara critiques the [lack of ideological diversity in the media](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), linking it to declining public trust in journalism and calling for greater balance in reporting.
This is the Mountain Minute for Tuesday, January 7, 2025.
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Congress certifies Trump’s 2024 election victory, clearing path to Oval Office](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [FOX NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Congress officially certified President-elect Trump’s 2024 election victory on Monday, taking him one step closer to assuming the Oval Office at the end of this month.
Tellers for the Senate’s vote counting were Sens. Deb Fischer, R-Neb., and Amy Klobuchar, D-Minn., and those on the House side were Reps. Bryan Steil, R-Wis., and Joe Morelle, D-N.Y.
The 312 electoral college votes for Trump were counted and presented by the tellers, in addition to the 226 votes for Vice President Kamala Harris.
It was the final step before Trump's inauguration on Jan. 20.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Brita Horn announces she will seek chair of Colorado Republican Party](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Former Routt County treasurer and fire chief Brita Horn has declared her intention to run for chair of the Colorado Republican Party, emphasizing unity and election victories in 2026.
* Horn’s campaign priorities include building relationships with candidates, donors, and activists, securing resources, and ensuring accountability within the party.
* A longtime grassroots leader, Horn has served as a Lincoln Club director, Maggie’s List chair, and county party vice-chair, with a history of public service in government and fire management.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Smart Wolf Policy group organizes to recall failed Colorado wolf law](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* The Smart Wolf Policy group has initiated a campaign to repeal Colorado’s controversial 2020 wolf reintroduction law, arguing that it has caused widespread harm to ranchers, wildlife, and the state’s economy.
* The group plans to submit its petition to the Secretary of State’s office by mid-March 2025 and will need to gather over 220,000 valid signatures within six months to bring the issue to voters in the 2026 election.
* Advocates emphasize the law’s negative impacts, including rising grocery prices, threats to food security, and ecological imbalances, while calling on all Coloradans to join the effort to overturn the legislation.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Letter: Reflections on America and the once thriving rural family farm economy](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* A farmer in Maybell, Colorado, shares insights into the challenges facing rural family farms, highlighting a shift away from traditional agricultural practices due to urbanization and modern labor trends.
* The letter critiques policies that prioritize urban development over rural sustainability, noting their contribution to housing affordability crises and the decline of small-scale farming.
* The author advocates for a renewed focus on supporting rural communities, emphasizing the need to balance urban and agricultural development for long-term economic and societal stability.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Gaines: You, too, can file a campaign finance complaint against someone](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_You may not have known this before, but you have the ability to accuse someone in this state of a campaign finance law violation._
_You don’t have to be a witness at a trial. In fact, once you make the accusation, you are essentially out of the process. You will get notifications from the secretary of state’s office about the progress of the complaint, but you don’t do anything other than swear out a complaint._
_You also don’t have to have an intimate knowledge of campaign finance law. I’m not urging you to make wild, uneducated accusations here, but you don’t need to be a lawyer or an expert. Many of the rules around campaign finance are pretty straightforward, thus finding violations doesn’t take an expert._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️**

Not featured but worth reading: As the approaches, Colorado is preparing for significant debates on issues ranging from health care reform to labor laws and environmental regulations. The upcoming session promises to impact nearly every sector of the state’s economy, including new challenges for businesses and efforts to reshape union policies. 🇺🇸On the National Stage... Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 presidential election during a joint session of Congress. A reflects on the decline of the American family farm and the societal shifts that have contributed to economic and cultural changes in rural areas. Additionally, Cory Gaines encourages citizens to hold politicians accountable by when violations are suspected. Lastly, an extra read: Jon Caldara critiques the , linking it to declining public trust in journalism and calling for greater balance in reporting. This is the Mountain Minute for Tuesday, January 7, 2025. – Heidi Ganahl | TODAY'S TOP STORIES Congress officially certified President-elect Trump’s 2024 election victory on Monday, taking him one step closer to assuming the Oval Office at the end of this month. Tellers for the Senate’s vote counting were Sens. Deb Fischer, R-Neb., and Amy Klobuchar, D-Minn., and those on the House side were Reps. Bryan Steil, R-Wis., and Joe Morelle, D-N.Y. Advocates emphasize the law’s negative impacts, including rising grocery prices, threats to food security, and ecological imbalances, while calling on all Coloradans to join the effort to overturn the legislation. | A farmer in Maybell, Colorado, shares insights into the challenges facing rural family farms, highlighting a shift away from traditional agricultural practices due to urbanization and modern labor trends. The letter critiques policies that prioritize urban development over rural sustainability, noting their contribution to housing affordability crises and the decline of small-scale farming. You also don’t have to have an intimate knowledge of campaign finance law. I’m not urging you to make wild, uneducated accusations here, but you don’t need to be a lawyer or an expert. name: Navigating Uncertainty (by Vikram Mansharamani)
subject: 25 Global Developments To Watch Over The Next Five Years
body:
Exploring the certainty of uncertainty in the years to come

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..opKWoHe77B1cudbU2QYqJdNZ6sHkl_zhUnnwZqA-LKQ?) for more

# [25 Global Developments To Watch Over The Next Five Years](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=154154003&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0MTU0MDAzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzYyNTQwODMsImV4cCI6MTczODg0NjA4MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.eFeoGaPHLY5Nn9C6cAB3JG1gvKmaJU6Ir3VHJYP2bqY)
### Exploring the certainty of uncertainty in the years to come
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Jan 7
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=154154003&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0MTU0MDAzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczNjI1NDA4MywiZXhwIjoxNzM4ODQ2MDgzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.rxHJAqNw7NGrdjkDo8lIjSm8QtiZ0Ml6tlr2QstT9l8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/528c906f-853f-420d-98a4-e80342d4a8a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
2024 was a year of confusing, inspiring, depressing, and energizing developments on many fronts. Politics remained front and center as billions of people selected new leaders in elections held in Taiwan, Pakistan, Indonesia, India, South Africa, Mexico, the UK, and the United States, as well as a dozens of others.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bb3a26fc-52fc-4d65-a29e-6075746b1db8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Source: https://www.pewresearch.org/global/2024/12/11/global-elections-in-2024-what-we-learned-in-a-year-of-political-disruption/](https://substack.com/redirect/664fdb8c-30f8-48d5-802e-b8d1342ef768?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
I took two main lessons from what _Time_ magazine has called the "ultimate election year." First, **it was a bad year for incumbents** as [voters blamed existing leadership for inflation and economic challenges](https://substack.com/redirect/62d064f0-6b29-4e62-8066-ca366feb27d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). New parties took power in the United States, the UK, Botswana, Ghana, Panama, Portugal and a host of other nations while the ruling parties in India, France, Japan and South Africa endured major setbacks.
Navigating Uncertainty is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LT21wdRnzXjn3eE_iJArcbdFyvKHQjazV8kB-p14ZAE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=154154003)
Second, the **forces of populism and nationalism are alive and well** around the world. Marine Le Pen's National Rally increased its influence in the French National Assembly while Austria's Freedom Party earned 29% of the vote in the September election. Trump and his MAGA movement won decisively in the United States while Claudia Scheinbaum channeled the popularity of the populist Morena Party to become Mexico's first female president. Narendra Modi and Prabowo Subianto demonstrated the populations of India and Indonesia, respectively, still seek strong nationalist leaders.
It was also interesting to note how [international conflicts](https://substack.com/redirect/81172e6c-7cd6-45d6-8626-fe94f2735ea8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) impacted domestic politics. In America, the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East were galvanizing forces that led to within party tensions as Republicans debated the merits of supporting Ukraine while Democrats were torn about denouncing Israel. Lest we think this is just an American phenomenon, similar dynamics unfolded in the United Kingdom, France, and Austria. Interestingly, thinking of China as hostile to Western values and interests is longer a contentious political issue but has emerged as one of the few areas of unity in America's otherwise polarized political domain.
The natural world also offered its share of noteworthy events. A total solar eclipse led to traffic jams and sold out hotels in remote areas of rural New England, the northern lights were visible beyond the poles, earthquakes rocked Taiwan and the NY/NJ area, wildfires spread in Greece and California, and hurricanes Helene and Milton wreaked havoc in America.
### **2024 was defined by the introduction of breakdancing as an Olympic sport, planes falling apart in flight, panic among pets in Springfield Ohio, drifting ships disabling bridges, a climate summit hosted in a petro-state, and the eating of a $6 million banana.**
Technology continued to accelerate. SpaceX caught its Super Heavy booster, artificial intelligence spread into domain of politics, quantum breakthroughs led to extraordinary excitement, and the [FDA approved the first ever personalized immunotherapy treatment targeting solid tumors](https://substack.com/redirect/83357b2d-d0cc-48ee-92f4-5c57e003115f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Technology-enabled decentralization marched forward in finance, energy, and education while big-tech companies acknowledged their power hungry ambitions by supporting nuclear energy.
And in the world of money, inflation slowed, interest rates began to fall, and the stock market surged. The labor market remained tight, consumer spending was resilient, and US debt levels rose to new records. Gold and bitcoin hit all-time highs as NVIDIA and quantum computing equities faced insatiable investor demand.
In a year that was defined by the introduction of breakdancing as an Olympic sport, planes falling apart in flight, panic among pets in Springfield Ohio, drifting ships disabling bridges, a climate summit hosted in a petro-state, and the eating of a $6 million banana, humorist Dave Berry reminds us not to forget technological risks of our highly interconnected reality:
> _[In other news, a massive worldwide internet disruption paralyzes global air travel, along with banks, hotels, hospitals and other industries, when Arnold A. Frinkledorp, an 87-year-old retiree who is attempting to send an email to his sister from his AOL account, accidentally presses the ALT, backslash, left arrow, F3, ampersand and right parenthesis keys simultaneously-- which apparently nobody has ever done before -- thereby triggering a Windows glitch that causes more than 8.5 million computers to crash. The disruption winds up costing businesses an estimated $5 billion, although, on the plus side, Mr. Frinkledorp's email -- a meme of a cat wearing sunglasses -- is successfully delivered to his sister, who accidentally deletes it.](https://substack.com/redirect/68bd3470-76c7-408f-80e4-6bc58e25419c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
As I've stressed before, **uncertainty and change are unavoidable realities of life.** Yet all of us are asked to make decisions in the face of these fluid dynamics. What's the best way to do so? Sadly, there is no one correct answer, but I believe we must look through the day-to-day noise to have any chance of identifying signals. Long-term trends that drive seemingly disconnected developments offer a compass to guide us through the numerous cross-currents that plague our lives; and as I've noted several times in the past, I believe this approach is at least marginally more useful than my **[Ouija board](https://substack.com/redirect/4e4e82b4-cefa-47cb-8fc9-455209c86a84?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** or **[Magic 8 ball](https://substack.com/redirect/da8e570d-9165-4f0e-9ece-509ff0a2a7b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
Unlike many others who tend to make predictions on a one-year view, I opt for a 5 year look as I believe time allows signals to surface amidst the ubiquitous noise. I've been doing this since 2012 and have publicly posted my predictions since January 2015. (Links: **[2015](https://substack.com/redirect/8a03763b-67b1-44d3-85eb-93ae8b7cf816?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[2016](https://substack.com/redirect/a51b6cbe-6692-4e2c-87ed-460c19179c7b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[2017](https://substack.com/redirect/9500287c-49ce-498c-99f3-76b4bd36f0f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[2018](https://substack.com/redirect/7865220f-c602-4268-8881-040046d562ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[2019](https://substack.com/redirect/5c8c7435-5204-442e-9994-2ed187c6cbfc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[2020](https://substack.com/redirect/cc453d94-3d90-4d3a-ae28-56c32908b799?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[2021](https://substack.com/redirect/31c112bf-ad0d-47f0-a2a6-51bd30816ad4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[2022](https://substack.com/redirect/e470ca20-36b6-4f77-b7fa-cf1c6ad51800?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) , [2023](https://substack.com/redirect/ba7127b5-825e-45da-9261-aacede039cd4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [2024](https://substack.com/redirect/2230f415-71c1-44ed-a6e4-84137035053a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))**. I'd encourage you to revisit any or all of them, not just to see how wrong (and sometimes right!) I can be, but perhaps also to notice how the mere act of considering alternatives can be constructive.
It's in the spirit of embracing uncertainty and thinking broadly that I offer you this year's set of global developments to watch over the next five years.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LT21wdRnzXjn3eE_iJArcbdFyvKHQjazV8kB-p14ZAE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=154154003)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/47d9cc47-d60a-4fe5-8c42-e56b2e95db6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
1. [Skyrocketing demand for artificial intelligence exposes decades of underinvestment in American power infrastructure](https://substack.com/redirect/5d74f97a-828a-4a9d-99fb-5453c30bf73f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). As [brownouts become increasingly common](https://substack.com/redirect/143f1b4b-0d77-4df6-93a5-10d9f3b6ef42?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), public sentiment towards technology companies turns hostile, driving them to rapidly deploy ([rather than merely invest in](https://substack.com/redirect/4566d2a0-b70e-4f9b-b94f-56783ad965ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) nuclear power. The price of uranium plunges as [breakthroughs in extracting uranium from seawater](https://substack.com/redirect/98e0313d-78a6-4e5c-9403-434136820157?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) more than offset surging demand for the commodity.
2. As a result of rising debt levels, extreme political polarization, growing income inequality, and the regular weaponization of the US dollar system, the world begins to abandon the US dollar as a global reserve currency. The US, in what may prove to be one of the most significant actions in thousands of years of economic history, decides to proactively accept the dollar's death and embraces bitcoin, a move that permanently denies every country the exorbitant privileges associated with having a global reserve currency. Countries with the most bitcoin per capita are enriched by the transition.
### Bitcoin...permanently denies every country the exorbitant privileges associated with having a global reserve currency.
3. Guam emerges as ground zero of the US China Cyberwar. [Chinese state-sponsored hacking groups continue to intensify their attacks on US critical infrastructure](https://substack.com/redirect/efa6c97d-b022-4ccf-acf0-ab012c71c76d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and a[fter taking down Guam's electricity grid and disrupting American military operations](https://substack.com/redirect/17d78421-4fdc-4aef-a05d-26177ff9eaf2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the US responds with a demonstration of overwhelming offensive cyber capabilities that releases evidence and data about the CCP's surveillance operations against its Chinese citizens onto every single Chinese cell phone. Anti-CCP protests simultaneously breakout in China's top 100 cities.
4. Evidence emerges that the world's leading militaries have all been developing [weather warfare](https://substack.com/redirect/bbfc3469-dab6-46ee-9db7-64bfce8fc8cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) capabilities. The movie _**[Geostorm](https://substack.com/redirect/4d2753da-01d5-4a05-822d-7e1c7d233dcc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ proves prophetic.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6a59326f-a3b0-415f-ad45-d3e0ac517c70?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
5. As surveillance capitalists continue to suck up data about users from every conceivable source and then use propensity scoring algorithms to nudge people into profit-generating actions, the threat to free will creates unprecedented interest in digital privacy technology. [Apostrophy becomes the world's hottest company](https://substack.com/redirect/c2044d5d-afa1-4719-be1c-bfdfd5ef418f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
6. As GLP-1 drugs gain effectiveness at reducing appetites, devastating side effects are exposed that force governments to stop subsidizing them. In the United States, [the Make American Healthy Again movement](https://substack.com/redirect/49f40678-384a-4848-a435-f107afd4da23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) leads to the widespread removal of dangerous chemicals from American food, leading to improved health outcomes. Americans continue to demand convenient and easy-to-prepare foods, even if they're "unhealthy." [Single serve frozen foods sales double](https://substack.com/redirect/828edb9b-98a0-41ed-becd-d1b7ea85014b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and global soup sales hit all time records; [The Campbell's Company comes to regret dropping the word "Soup" from their official name after more than 150 years](https://substack.com/redirect/ef3cf56d-2d20-43ad-8b31-f5f2e9a53d60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### The Campbell's Company comes to regret dropping the word "Soup" from their official name after more than 150 years.
7. The [US abandons its global concerns and opts instead to focus on the Western Hemisphere](https://substack.com/redirect/96798d8e-07f3-45ac-8df6-29f790fee3ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The global media rapidly labels this new foreign policy stance as the Trump Doctrine, which is a _de facto_ resurrection of the Monroe Doctrine, and clearly telegraphs to China and Russia that Latin America is off limits. [The Venezuela-Guyana conflict over oil becomes a flashpoint in this new dynamic](https://substack.com/redirect/8b7ba2bf-c493-476a-84da-93b798e1337d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), one that rapidly deescalates when [Panama voluntarily sells the Panama Canal Zone back to the United States in return for American security guarantees](https://substack.com/redirect/9846b585-19d5-4e25-9795-d1ca4480ccd1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Because of its fuzzy geography from a hemispheric perspective, [tensions rise in the Arctic](https://substack.com/redirect/31e6254e-915c-4a00-aff8-c7dd54a60e28?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
8. As the [Department of Government Efficiency](https://substack.com/redirect/658e7235-201c-4699-b906-9a49213c13cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) suggests they will seek to end the government's heavy use of cost-plus contracting (a dynamic that has supported and protected large defense contractors), hundreds of enterprising Boeing engineers and executives leave the beleaguered aerospace giant and start dozens of world changing companies.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LT21wdRnzXjn3eE_iJArcbdFyvKHQjazV8kB-p14ZAE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=154154003)
9. [The Advanced Regenerative Manufacturing Institute in Manchester NH](https://substack.com/redirect/0be2dd09-253a-48a4-a70f-8100fe82ac5f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) becomes the most important [tissue engineering](https://substack.com/redirect/c89450b6-31de-4236-95c3-4128a3e3ea0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) effort in the world and begins demonstrating the power of growing organs to solve medical problems rather than using pharmaceuticals to address symptoms.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3f6b5e54-94f0-4f61-993c-fa1d0c122db9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
10. The largest economies in the world continue to get older as birth rates fall and lifespans expand. After years of stagnation, AARP membership swells to more than 40 million people. Governments around the world create departments and committees to address policies related Aging. Alongside this dynamic, Millennials and Zoomers demand a radical restructuring of entitlement programs such as Social Security and Medicare. The cross-currents lead to policy proposals such as a wholesale replacement of the income tax with a consumption tax, a development that unleashes an American economic boom.
11. President Trump and the Republican-led Congress act decisively to revive and pass [The Sunshine Protection Act](https://substack.com/redirect/c4e10ffd-1f97-4089-8eb8-97223c5d2069?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a law that makes daylight savings time permanent and ends the twice-a-year, sleep-disrupting clock adjustments that [CNN has suggested are racist](https://substack.com/redirect/ded014cf-0d14-41bf-baa0-6aaadaac02d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
12. Sleep deprivation is declared a public health emergency as analysis of personal data (supposedly anonymized by Apple, Fitbit, Garmin, and Oura) convincingly links [many accidents, errors, and medical ailments to inadequate sleep](https://substack.com/redirect/e271ea09-26a0-4888-bf3a-e6cdefa2d064?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). New laws emerge that require [LED light manufacturers to disclose sleep disruption risks](https://substack.com/redirect/07be69d2-b774-4ebc-838f-4aa271ce6080?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Mathew Walker's _**[WHY WE SLEEP: Unlocking the Power of Sleep and Dreams](https://substack.com/redirect/1832915a-b6e1-4fdd-a7c2-8564b9ee0952?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ becomes required reading in most medical schools and residency programs prohibit aspiring doctors from working shifts longer than 12 hours.
### Sleep deprivation is declared a public health emergency as analysis of personal data (supposedly anonymized by Apple, Fitbit, Garmin, and Oura) suggests many accidents, errors, and medical ailments are linked to inadequate sleep.
13. [Commercial activities in space surge amidst increasing belief that humans will be an interplanetary species. Many terrestrial communications capabilities (data centers, cell towers, etc.) are moved to low Earth orbit](https://substack.com/redirect/c7c4de4e-edd4-4b1b-9cbf-63aed562c641?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). As lunar mining technologies improve, [a Moon Rush draws investors and entrepreneurs in pursuit of gold](https://substack.com/redirect/0f4e2945-7c34-4977-96a0-4a646591bff8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [helium-3 and other commodities](https://substack.com/redirect/24838e71-a3af-4b6e-b9f8-2868d27cca3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Alien and UFO disclosures accelerate from major governments, most of which are dismissed as "narrative control efforts" or psychological operations. No credible evidence of alien technology emerges.
14. Rapid advancements in the use and capabilities of autonomous [underwater drone swarms](https://substack.com/redirect/59969d6b-0c5c-4409-bdbd-8cbc9a54f256?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) effectively eliminates the strategic value and stealth of the most technologically advanced submarines. After one such swarm run by the PLA Navy embarrassingly forces an Australian submarine to surface in the Yellow Sea and is boarded by Chinese military personnel, American and British politicians call for an immediate termination of the [AUKUS partnership](https://substack.com/redirect/f55fc8fc-cd5b-40ca-8e81-248b150a5561?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In its place, the US agrees to sell 10,000 Manta Ray drones to Australia, [creating panic within PLA leadership](https://substack.com/redirect/3464cdba-785f-4f06-8e10-f3dd980e3003?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and euphoria among [Northrop Grumman shareholders](https://substack.com/redirect/82537376-31ab-4c1e-a9f2-2bd6fad217c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[](https://substack.com/redirect/fd9744fa-a042-4f67-8e9c-503863c1db12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
15. After a mass deportation of illegal immigrants, America passes [comprehensive immigration reform](https://substack.com/redirect/f9c49af8-d72e-44dc-bda3-92cf0bda762c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that prioritizes the American worker but also enables businesses to secure much-needed talent. Anti-immigrant sentiment dissipates and [America restores its global image as a shining city on a hill](https://substack.com/redirect/5d41dd65-4805-4828-8b6b-d4f465044a38?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LT21wdRnzXjn3eE_iJArcbdFyvKHQjazV8kB-p14ZAE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=154154003)
16. High school students and their tuition-paying parents increasingly [question the value of a liberal arts education from an elite institution](https://substack.com/redirect/9a351907-d2f5-4610-af45-24c187a13de1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). An unprecedented number of high school graduates enter the trades, motivated in part by Mike Rowe's [Dirty Jobs](https://substack.com/redirect/4ec9ce87-41f8-41b0-b877-be15c46a624e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). [Public opinion turns against the Ivy League and their prioritization of elites over national interests](https://substack.com/redirect/c34cc81f-45dd-4792-8eab-aafcca49a51c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), leading politicians to threaten their tax exempt status.
### Public opinion turns against the Ivy League and their prioritization of elites over national interests, leading politicians to threaten their tax exempt status.
17. In[ an acknowledgment that global multilateral institutions no longer work](https://substack.com/redirect/4d175831-ba78-41d0-bac1-08156eb1ce59?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), leaders from more than 50 nations suggest the United Nations should be disbanded.
18. Academics and practitioners alike conclude that a large pool of low-priced and young workers no longer provides an economic benefit to an emerging nation. Some go further to suggest that unemployed youth are a liability to governments that must figure out how to create jobs for them in a world that increasingly utilizes technology and automated manufacturing instead of unskilled labor. India debates implementing a one-child policy.
19. The world achieves peak climate alarmism and begins to question the motivations of the carbon control efforts. [Climate change conferences are increasingly hosted by petro-states](https://substack.com/redirect/c393d24f-0068-4d7f-b9d6-8f59c2c53fda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) eager to gain favor with global elites who, despite flying carbon-spewing private jets, seek the virtue-signaling benefits of attending such events. Guyana's president Dr. Irfan Ali's exposure of climate hypocrisy inspires other nations to push back against catastrophizing by elites; Bjorn Lomberg's _**[False Alarm: How Climate Change Panic Costs Us Trillions, Hurts the Poor, and Fails to Fix the Planet](https://substack.com/redirect/4d0687e9-4845-4829-bf10-250e350115b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ develops a cult-like-following among emerging countries with abundant hydrocarbon reserves.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/71cb4eab-5d36-478b-99e8-527d7610b1b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
20. [The Chinese gender imbalance](https://substack.com/redirect/e18cd912-a21b-4d4e-96ff-05d8abc440d6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) leads to a large pool of spouseless men who become disgruntled and restive. To minimize the perceived threat to stability, the CCP launches a development assistance program that grants interest-free loans to countries with more females than males, with a requirement they offer residency and work visas to Chinese males. [Moldova, Latvia, Armenia, Georgia, Belarus, Lithuania, Tonga, and Serbia](https://substack.com/redirect/1413c077-aa15-48a6-b6a1-bd64a043dbda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) immediately sign up for the program.
21. An increasingly competitive dynamic emerges among contenders seeking to succeed President Donald Trump, splitting the Republican Party into two factions (MAGA vs. MAHA?) that each threaten to leave and start their own party. On the back of such infighting, Democrat strategists salivate at the possibility of a midterm "Blue Wave" that fails to materialize.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LT21wdRnzXjn3eE_iJArcbdFyvKHQjazV8kB-p14ZAE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=154154003)
22. Private equity begins to feel the pinch of higher interest rates and starts investing at lower multiples, leading to disappointing returns for prior vintages. Holding companies and conglomerates with the ability to redeploy cash flow into the most promising opportunities emerge as the most productive return generators.
### Holding companies and conglomerates with the ability to redeploy cash flow into the most promising opportunities emerge as the most productive return generators.
23. Persistent work from home pressures continue to be a headwind for commercial real estate, specifically office buildings. Despite the acknowledged loss of productivity and employer demands that workers return to the office, large employers find the tight labor market prevents material changes in work from home practices which seem to be set. A sudden and rapid adoption of augmented and virtual reality headsets increases work-from-home productivity as managers are better able to monitor employee engagement.
24. The world increasingly bifurcates into [countries that respect individuals and those that prioritize the community or group](https://substack.com/redirect/757a1f01-e259-44f3-aba9-31cde95f7e6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). This individualist-collectivist divide leads to a resurgence of interest in the ideas of _Atlas Shrugged_. Standing out and producing become the gold standards of measuring usefulness in individualist societies while blending in and not-rocking-the-boat is highly valued in collectivist societies.
25. Corporate America abandons stakeholder capitalism en masse and embraces shareholder capitalism in a move that revitalizes innovation and productivity. DEI goals, ESG metrics other non-economic considerations are acknowledged as being _de facto_ socialist in objective and are removed from the criteria used by investors and boards to evaluate management performance.

Each year, I end my predictions with the prescient words of John Kenneth Galbraith, who eloquently captures the essence of forecasters. There are, he notes, two types: "those who don't know and those who don't know they don't know." Feel free to decide which you think I am, but I do hope that these 25 possibilities are **[useful in spurring your thoughts](https://substack.com/redirect/311a7921-e285-41f3-990f-7b34ef119515?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
And if you find today's overwhelming uncertainty distressing, don't! Remember that uncertainty and opportunity are two sides of the same coin -- you can't have one without the other! I'll leave you with an excerpt from Fleetwood Mac's hit song "Don't Stop" which I often have conference organizers play as my "walking up on stage" music.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c584af2a-5fbe-426e-9f5e-b05f03643e2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Best wishes for a happy, healthy, and productive 2025!
Regards,
#### _Vikram_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LT21wdRnzXjn3eE_iJArcbdFyvKHQjazV8kB-p14ZAE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=154154003)
| | POLL
---
What do you think of Dr. Mansharamani's piece "25 Global Developments To Watch Over The Next Five Years"?
| [Thought-provoking and useful](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.G7l-VhI46s9ahUg9t_FKKKTdjKF1DEylYu7sWVuPb5g?)
---
[Interesting but not helpful](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.QuEdzJSodOT90k1GrPaRsBgh0eURdQYjyRLlhzcK-nM?)
[Complete waste of time ](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.Y2mOwTaCYm7pSz9_i26JZiRk_gU7mPRrcLsLgj4nePA?)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/11c7b7e9-0ae2-4b1d-a7f5-02085719eef3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/e2b956a2-ec65-4898-90fa-7f39bb46ef50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/1315813c-bce2-42df-863f-481dfc3b159a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/3c70fc1a-e1a9-49e2-a148-8ac36b69785f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.
_**Navigating Uncertainty**_ is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

clean_body: Exploring the certainty of uncertainty in the years to come | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Exploring the certainty of uncertainty in the years to come | | | Jan 7 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- 2024 was a year of confusing, inspiring, depressing, and energizing developments on many fronts. Politics remained front and center as billions of people selected new leaders in elections held in Taiwan, Pakistan, Indonesia, India, South Africa, Mexico, the UK, and the United States, as well as a dozens of others. | | ---|---|--- I took two main lessons from what Time magazine has called the "ultimate election year." First, it was a bad year for incumbents as . New parties took power in the United States, the UK, Botswana, Ghana, Panama, Portugal and a host of other nations while the ruling parties in India, France, Japan and South Africa endured major setbacks. Navigating Uncertainty is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. Second, the forces of populism and nationalism are alive and well around the world. Marine Le Pen's National Rally increased its influence in the French National Assembly while Austria's Freedom Party earned 29% of the vote in the September election. Trump and his MAGA movement won decisively in the United States while Claudia Scheinbaum channeled the popularity of the populist Morena Party to become Mexico's first female president. Narendra Modi and Prabowo Subianto demonstrated the populations of India and Indonesia, respectively, still seek strong nationalist leaders. It was also interesting to note how impacted domestic politics. In America, the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East were galvanizing forces that led to within party tensions as Republicans debated the merits of supporting Ukraine while Democrats were torn about denouncing Israel. Lest we think this is just an American phenomenon, similar dynamics unfolded in the United Kingdom, France, and Austria. Interestingly, thinking of China as hostile to Western values and interests is longer a contentious political issue but has emerged as one of the few areas of unity in America's otherwise polarized political domain. The natural world also offered its share of noteworthy events. A total solar eclipse led to traffic jams and sold out hotels in remote areas of rural New England, the northern lights were visible beyond the poles, earthquakes rocked Taiwan and the NY/NJ area, wildfires spread in Greece and California, and hurricanes Helene and Milton wreaked havoc in America. 2024 was defined by the introduction of breakdancing as an Olympic sport, planes falling apart in flight, panic among pets in Springfield Ohio, drifting ships disabling bridges, a climate summit hosted in a petro-state, and the eating of a $6 million banana. Technology continued to accelerate. SpaceX caught its Super Heavy booster, artificial intelligence spread into domain of politics, quantum breakthroughs led to extraordinary excitement, and the . Technology-enabled decentralization marched forward in finance, energy, and education while big-tech companies acknowledged their power hungry ambitions by supporting nuclear energy. And in the world of money, inflation slowed, interest rates began to fall, and the stock market surged. The labor market remained tight, consumer spending was resilient, and US debt levels rose to new records. Gold and bitcoin hit all-time highs as NVIDIA and quantum computing equities faced insatiable investor demand. In a year that was defined by the introduction of breakdancing as an Olympic sport, planes falling apart in flight, panic among pets in Springfield Ohio, drifting ships disabling bridges, a climate summit hosted in a petro-state, and the eating of a $6 million banana, humorist Dave Berry reminds us not to forget technological risks of our highly interconnected reality: As I've stressed before, uncertainty and change are unavoidable realities of life. Yet all of us are asked to make decisions in the face of these fluid dynamics. What's the best way to do so? Sadly, there is no one correct answer, but I believe we must look through the day-to-day noise to have any chance of identifying signals. Long-term trends that drive seemingly disconnected developments offer a compass to guide us through the numerous cross-currents that plague our lives; and as I've noted several times in the past, I believe this approach is at least marginally more useful than my or . Unlike many others who tend to make predictions on a one-year view, I opt for a 5 year look as I believe time allows signals to surface amidst the ubiquitous noise. I've been doing this since 2012 and have publicly posted my predictions since January 2015. (Links: , , , , , , , , , ). I'd encourage you to revisit any or all of them, not just to see how wrong (and sometimes right!) I can be, but perhaps also to notice how the mere act of considering alternatives can be constructive. It's in the spirit of embracing uncertainty and thinking broadly that I offer you this year's set of global developments to watch over the next five years. | | ---|---|--- 1. . As , public sentiment towards technology companies turns hostile, driving them to rapidly deploy () nuclear power. The price of uranium plunges as more than offset surging demand for the commodity. 2. As a result of rising debt levels, extreme political polarization, growing income inequality, and the regular weaponization of the US dollar system, the world begins to abandon the US dollar as a global reserve currency. The US, in what may prove to be one of the most significant actions in thousands of years of economic history, decides to proactively accept the dollar's death and embraces bitcoin, a move that permanently denies every country the exorbitant privileges associated with having a global reserve currency. Countries with the most bitcoin per capita are enriched by the transition. Bitcoin...permanently denies every country the exorbitant privileges associated with having a global reserve currency. 3. Guam emerges as ground zero of the US China Cyberwar. , and a, the US responds with a demonstration of overwhelming offensive cyber capabilities that releases evidence and data about the CCP's surveillance operations against its Chinese citizens onto every single Chinese cell phone. Anti-CCP protests simultaneously breakout in China's top 100 cities. 4. Evidence emerges that the world's leading militaries have all been developing capabilities. The movie proves prophetic. 5. As surveillance capitalists continue to suck up data about users from every conceivable source and then use propensity scoring algorithms to nudge people into profit-generating actions, the threat to free will creates unprecedented interest in digital privacy technology. . 6. As GLP-1 drugs gain effectiveness at reducing appetites, devastating side effects are exposed that force governments to stop subsidizing them. In the United States, leads to the widespread removal of dangerous chemicals from American food, leading to improved health outcomes. Americans continue to demand convenient and easy-to-prepare foods, even if they're "unhealthy." , and global soup sales hit all time records; . The Campbell's Company comes to regret dropping the word "Soup" from their official name after more than 150 years. 7. The . The global media rapidly labels this new foreign policy stance as the Trump Doctrine, which is a de facto resurrection of the Monroe Doctrine, and clearly telegraphs to China and Russia that Latin America is off limits. , one that rapidly deescalates when . Because of its fuzzy geography from a hemispheric perspective, . 8. As the suggests they will seek to end the government's heavy use of cost-plus contracting (a dynamic that has supported and protected large defense contractors), hundreds of enterprising Boeing engineers and executives leave the beleaguered aerospace giant and start dozens of world changing companies. 9. becomes the most important effort in the world and begins demonstrating the power of growing organs to solve medical problems rather than using pharmaceuticals to address symptoms. 10. The largest economies in the world continue to get older as birth rates fall and lifespans expand. After years of stagnation, AARP membership swells to more than 40 million people. Governments around the world create departments and committees to address policies related Aging. Alongside this dynamic, Millennials and Zoomers demand a radical restructuring of entitlement programs such as Social Security and Medicare. The cross-currents lead to policy proposals such as a wholesale replacement of the income tax with a consumption tax, a development that unleashes an American economic boom. 11. President Trump and the Republican-led Congress act decisively to revive and pass , a law that makes daylight savings time permanent and ends the twice-a-year, sleep-disrupting clock adjustments that . 12. Sleep deprivation is declared a public health emergency as analysis of personal data (supposedly anonymized by Apple, Fitbit, Garmin, and Oura) convincingly links . New laws emerge that require . Mathew Walker's becomes required reading in most medical schools and residency programs prohibit aspiring doctors from working shifts longer than 12 hours. Sleep deprivation is declared a public health emergency as analysis of personal data (supposedly anonymized by Apple, Fitbit, Garmin, and Oura) suggests many accidents, errors, and medical ailments are linked to inadequate sleep. 13. . As lunar mining technologies improve, , . Alien and UFO disclosures accelerate from major governments, most of which are dismissed as "narrative control efforts" or psychological operations. No credible evidence of alien technology emerges. 14. Rapid advancements in the use and capabilities of autonomous effectively eliminates the strategic value and stealth of the most technologically advanced submarines. After one such swarm run by the PLA Navy embarrassingly forces an Australian submarine to surface in the Yellow Sea and is boarded by Chinese military personnel, American and British politicians call for an immediate termination of the . In its place, the US agrees to sell 10,000 Manta Ray drones to Australia, and euphoria among . 15. After a mass deportation of illegal immigrants, America passes that prioritizes the American worker but also enables businesses to secure much-needed talent. Anti-immigrant sentiment dissipates and . 16. High school students and their tuition-paying parents increasingly . An unprecedented number of high school graduates enter the trades, motivated in part by Mike Rowe's . , leading politicians to threaten their tax exempt status. Public opinion turns against the Ivy League and their prioritization of elites over national interests, leading politicians to threaten their tax exempt status. 17. In, leaders from more than 50 nations suggest the United Nations should be disbanded. 18. Academics and practitioners alike conclude that a large pool of low-priced and young workers no longer provides an economic benefit to an emerging nation. Some go further to suggest that unemployed youth are a liability to governments that must figure out how to create jobs for them in a world that increasingly utilizes technology and automated manufacturing instead of unskilled labor. India debates implementing a one-child policy. 19. The world achieves peak climate alarmism and begins to question the motivations of the carbon control efforts. eager to gain favor with global elites who, despite flying carbon-spewing private jets, seek the virtue-signaling benefits of attending such events. Guyana's president Dr. Irfan Ali's exposure of climate hypocrisy inspires other nations to push back against catastrophizing by elites; Bjorn Lomberg's develops a cult-like-following among emerging countries with abundant hydrocarbon reserves. 20. leads to a large pool of spouseless men who become disgruntled and restive. To minimize the perceived threat to stability, the CCP launches a development assistance program that grants interest-free loans to countries with more females than males, with a requirement they offer residency and work visas to Chinese males. immediately sign up for the program. 21. An increasingly competitive dynamic emerges among contenders seeking to succeed President Donald Trump, splitting the Republican Party into two factions (MAGA vs. MAHA?) that each threaten to leave and start their own party. On the back of such infighting, Democrat strategists salivate at the possibility of a midterm "Blue Wave" that fails to materialize. 22. Private equity begins to feel the pinch of higher interest rates and starts investing at lower multiples, leading to disappointing returns for prior vintages. Holding companies and conglomerates with the ability to redeploy cash flow into the most promising opportunities emerge as the most productive return generators. Holding companies and conglomerates with the ability to redeploy cash flow into the most promising opportunities emerge as the most productive return generators. 23. Persistent work from home pressures continue to be a headwind for commercial real estate, specifically office buildings. Despite the acknowledged loss of productivity and employer demands that workers return to the office, large employers find the tight labor market prevents material changes in work from home practices which seem to be set. A sudden and rapid adoption of augmented and virtual reality headsets increases work-from-home productivity as managers are better able to monitor employee engagement. 24. The world increasingly bifurcates into . This individualist-collectivist divide leads to a resurgence of interest in the ideas of Atlas Shrugged. Standing out and producing become the gold standards of measuring usefulness in individualist societies while blending in and not-rocking-the-boat is highly valued in collectivist societies. 25. Corporate America abandons stakeholder capitalism en masse and embraces shareholder capitalism in a move that revitalizes innovation and productivity. DEI goals, ESG metrics other non-economic considerations are acknowledged as being de facto socialist in objective and are removed from the criteria used by investors and boards to evaluate management performance. Each year, I end my predictions with the prescient words of John Kenneth Galbraith, who eloquently captures the essence of forecasters. There are, he notes, two types: "those who don't know and those who don't know they don't know." Feel free to decide which you think I am, but I do hope that these 25 possibilities are . And if you find today's overwhelming uncertainty distressing, don't! Remember that uncertainty and opportunity are two sides of the same coin -- you can't have one without the other! I'll leave you with an excerpt from Fleetwood Mac's hit song "Don't Stop" which I often have conference organizers play as my "walking up on stage" music. Best wishes for a happy, healthy, and productive 2025! Regards, Vikram | | POLL What do you think of Dr. Mansharamani's piece "25 Global Developments To Watch Over The Next Five Years"? | ? ? ? | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and . Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. Navigating Uncertainty is a reader-supported publication.
